title: Creamy Mushroom Pasta

ingredients.name: fresh button or cremini mushrooms, sliced

subs.name: zucchini and capsicum, diced
subs.notes: sauté the vegetables the same way; cook zucchini 2 minutes less to retain texture

ingredients.name: fresh cream (heavy cream or cooking cream)

subs.name: 200 ml full-fat coconut milk or thick cashew cream
subs.notes: coconut milk adds a slight sweetness; both make it dairy-free

subs.name: extra olive oil or a vegan spread
subs.notes: butter adds richness; use coconut oil for a fully plant-based dish

subs.name: nutritional yeast (2 tbsp) for a vegan version
subs.notes: adds a nutty, cheesy flavour without dairy

steps.text: Cook the pasta in a large pot of well-salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve a mug of pasta water before draining and set aside.
Heat the butter and olive oil together in a wide pan over medium-high heat. Add the mushrooms in a single layer — resist stirring for 2–3 minutes so they develop a golden sear rather than steaming.

Once the mushrooms are golden and their liquid has evaporated, push them to one side and add the onion. Cook for 3 minutes until soft, then stir everything together with the garlic and thyme. Fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Season with salt and black pepper, then pour in the cream. Stir and let it bubble on medium heat for 2–3 minutes until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon.

Add the drained pasta to the pan and toss, adding pasta water a little at a time to loosen the sauce until every piece of pasta is coated. Scatter over the grated cheese and half the parsley, toss once more.
Serve in warm bowls, topped with the remaining parsley and an extra grind of black pepper.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cream → 200 ml full-fat coconut milk or thick cashew cream (coconut milk adds a slight sweetness; both make it dairy-free); mushroom → zucchini and capsicum, diced (sauté the vegetables the same way; cook zucchini 2 minutes less to retain texture); cheese → nutritional yeast (2 tbsp) for a vegan version (adds a nutty, cheesy flavour without dairy); butter → extra olive oil or a vegan spread (butter adds richness; use coconut oil for a fully plant-based dish)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Amul fresh cream is the ideal substitute for cooking cream and is widely available. Sauté the mushrooms in a large kadai on high flame — the wide base helps them brown without stewing. If fresh Parmesan is unavailable, any processed cheese block grated finely adds a pleasant saltiness. Dried thyme is sold in the spice section at most supermarkets.
